text: - (b) In  the  event  that  Contractor  delays,  disrupts,  hinders,  or  interferes  with  the  Subcontract  Work, Contractor shall owe Subcontractor therefor only an extension of time for completion equal to the delay caused, and then  only  if  written  claim  is  made  to  Contractor  within  forty-eight  (48)  hours  from  the  beginning  of  the  delay, disruption,  hindrance  or  interference.  Subcontractor  shall  not  be  entitled  to  any  claim  for  damages  or  increased costs occasioned by any such delay. If this Paragraph 31(b) is found to be invalid or unenforceable under applicable law, any liability of Contractor to Subcontractor for any damages or increased costs occasioned by any such delay shall  be  limited  to  Subcontractor's  jobsite  overhead  for  the  period  of  delay,  and  under  no  circumstances  shall Contractor be liable  for  any  other  costs  or  damages  incurred  by  Subcontractor,  including  but  not  limited  to  any claims for home office overhead, lost productivity, lost business or profits, price increases, or any sub-subcontractor costs or damages as a result of any such delay.
